        "Monocular Open Vocabulary Occupancy Prediction for Indoor Scenes",
        "LineGraph2Road: Structural Graph Reasoning on Line Graphs for Road Network Extraction",
        "Latent Gaussian Splatting for 4D Panoptic Occupancy Tracking",
    ],
    "3D Vision / Reconstruction / Gaussian Splatting": [
        "GSTurb: Gaussian Splatting for Atmospheric Turbulence Mitigation",
        "Sapling-NeRF: Geo-Localised Sapling Reconstruction in Forests for Ecological Monitoring",
        "SwiftNDC: Fast Neural Depth Correction for High-Fidelity 3D Reconstruction",
        "BetterScene: 3D Scene Synthesis with Representation-Aligned Generative Model",
        "Multiprojective Geometry of Compatible Triples of Fundamental and Essential Matrices",
        "QuadSync: Quadrifocal Tensor Synchronization via Tucker Decomposition",
    ],
    "Others": [
        "SPMamba-YOLO: An underwater object detection network based on multi-scale feature enhancement and global context modeling",
        "Moral Preferences of LLMs Under Directed Contextual Influence",
        "Image-Based Classification of Olive Species Specific to Türkiye with Deep Neural Networks",
        "MovieTeller: Tool-augmented Movie Synopsis with ID Consistent Progressive Abstraction",
        "Partial recovery of meter-scale surface weather",
        "SkillNet: Create, Evaluate, and Connect AI Skills",
        "SO3UFormer: Learning Intrinsic Spherical Features for Rotation-Robust Panoramic Segmentation",
        "Self-Attention And Beyond the Infinite: Towards Linear Transformers with Infinite Self-Attention",
        "CGSA: Class-Guided Slot-Aware Adaptation for Source-Free Object Detection",
        "Modelling and Simulation of Neuromorphic Datasets for Anomaly Detection in Computer Vision",
        "Reflectance Multispectral Imaging for Soil Composition Estimation and USDA Texture Classification",
        "Small Object Detection Model with Spatial Laplacian Pyramid Attention and Multi-Scale Features Enhancement in Aerial Images",
        "Multidimensional Task Learning: A Unified Tensor Framework for Computer Vision Tasks",
        "Don't let the information slip away",
        "D-FINE-SEG: Object Detection and Instance Segmentation Framework with Multi-Backend Deployment",
        "Through BrokenEyes: How Eye Disorders Impact Face Detection?",
        "Pix2Key: Controllable Open-Vocabulary Retrieval with Semantic Decomposition and Self-Supervised Visual Dictionary Learning",
        "Sensor Generalization for Adaptive Sensing in Event-based Object Detection via Joint Distribution Training",
        "WARM-CAT: Warm-Started Test-Time Comprehensive Knowledge Accumulation for Compositional Zero-Shot Learning",
    ],
}

# Verify all 65 are classified
total_classified = sum(len(v) for v in classification.values())
print(f"Total classified: {total_classified}")

# Verify no duplicates
all_titles_classified = []
for v in classification.values():
    all_titles_classified.extend(v)
print(f"Unique titles: {len(set(all_titles_classified))}")

# Check for any missing
original_titles_set = set(correct_titles.values())
classified_set = set(all_titles_classified)
missing = original_titles_set - classified_set
extra = classified_set - original_titles_set
if missing:
    print(f"MISSING: {missing}")
if extra:
    print(f"EXTRA: {extra}")

# Now write the markdown
md = "# Paper Digest\n\n"

# Section 1: Rename Mapping
md += "### Rename Mapping\n"
md += "| Original Filename | New Filename |\n"
md += "|---|---|\n"
for orig in sorted(rename_map.keys()):
    md += f"| {orig} | {rename_map[orig]} |\n"
md += "\n"

# Section 2: Classification
md += "### Classification\n"
for category, titles in classification.items():
    md += f"#### {category}\n"
    for t in titles:
        md += f"- {t}\n"
    md += "\n"

# Section 3: Caption-Related Papers
md += "### Caption-Related Papers\n\n"

# CapRL
md += "#### CapRL: Stimulating Dense Image Caption Capabilities via Reinforcement Learning\n\n"
md += "**Table 2: Ablation on image sources. We annotate the images in ShareGPT4V-1M and DenseFusion-1M using CapRL-3B, and use them respectively as pretraining datasets for comparison.**\n\n"
md += "| Pretraining Dataset | Info VQA | Doc VQA | Chart QA | Real WorldQA | Math Vista | SEED2 Plus | MME RW | MMB | MMStar | MMVet | AI2D | GQA | Average |\n"
md += "|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|\n"
md += "| Vanilla | 43.9 | 81.0 | 72.7 | 55.1 | 41.6 | 56.6 | 30.5 | 68.6 | 44.7 | 41.0 | 68.3 | 61.5 | 55.5 |\n"
md += "| ShareGPT4V-1M | 46.1 | 82.4 | 74.2 | 55.0 | 44.7 | 60.5 | 29.8 | 68.9 | 45.2 | 42.4 | 70.1 | 61.4 | 56.7 |\n"
md += "| CapRL-ShareGPT4V-1M | 52.1 | 85.9 | 75.2 | 56.3 | 45.6 | 60.0 | 30.9 | 70.9 | 46.7 | 47.5 | 71.4 | 61.7 | 58.7 |\n"
md += "| DenseFusion-1M | 49.4 | 84.6 | 74.4 | 54.1 | 44.6 | 59.1 | 30.7 | 69.0 | 45.6 | 40.2 | 70.4 | 62.5 | 57.1 |\n"
md += "| CapRL-DenseFusion-1M | 55.0 | 87.8 | 77.5 | 56.2 | 44.7 | 62.8 | 32.0 | 71.0 | 46.6 | 49.9 | 72.7 | 62.3 | 59.9 |\n"
md += "\n"

# Asymmetric Idiosyncrasies
md += "#### Asymmetric Idiosyncrasies in Multimodal Models\n\n"
md += "**Table 2: Top distinctive TF-IDF phrases for each captioning model (generic appearance descriptors removed).**\n\n"
md += "| Rank | Claude-3.5-Sonnet | Gemini-1.5-Pro | GPT-4o | Qwen3-VL |\n"
md += "|---|---|---|---|---|\n"
md += "| 1 | lighting suggests | overall impression | image depicts | detailed description |\n"
md += "| 2 | visible background | low resolution | image features | high contrast |\n"
md += "| 3 | light colored | slightly low angle | person wearing | central focus |\n"
md += "| 4 | appears taken | close slightly low | handwritten digit | main subject |\n"
md += "| 5 | photo taken | eye level view | lush green | depth field |\n"
md += "| 6 | lighting creates | high angle view | sunny day | shallow depth field |\n"
md += "| 7 | depth field | light gray | clear blue | black background digit |\n"
md += "| 8 | composition creates | partially visible | handwritten number | white number |\n"
md += "| 9 | overall composition | overall lighting | setting appears | visible elements |\n"
md += "| 10 | scene appears | slightly high angle | partially visible | image captures |\n"
md += "\n"

# Cytoarchitecture in Words
md += "#### Cytoarchitecture in Words: Weakly Supervised Vision-Language Modeling for Human Brain Microscopy\n\n"
md += "**Note: This paper contains only one table (Table 1). There is no Table 2 in this paper.**\n\n"
md += "Table 1 shows the performance of LLMs on the created QA dataset for cytoarchitecture, reporting the share of correctly answered multiple choice questions out of 10,955.\n\n"
md += "| Model | # Parameters | Medical fine-tuning? | Score |\n"
md += "|---|---|---|---|\n"
md += "| First author performance | n.a. | n.a. | 34.0% |\n"
md += "| BioGPT | 0.35b | ✓ | 22.7% |\n"
md += "| BioGPT-Large | 1.5b | ✓ | 24.5% |\n"
md += "| PMC Llama 7b | 7b | ✓ | 28.1% |\n"
md += "| Llama-3-8b | 8b | ✗ | 58.1% |\n"
md += "| Ministral 8b | 8b | ✗ | 50.2% |\n"
md += "| Llama-3-8b Ultramedical | 8b | ✓ | 47.5% |\n"
md += "| Phi-4 | 14b | ✗ | 57.2% |\n"
md += "\n"

# ViCLIP-OT
md += "#### ViCLIP-OT: The First Foundation Vision-Language Model for Vietnamese Image-Text Retrieval with Optimal Transport\n\n"
md += "**Table 2: Zero-shot image–text retrieval results on KTVIC and Crossmodal-3600. KTVIC images are deduplicated against the UIT-OpenViIC training set. Vietnamese captions are used for Crossmodal-3600. ViCLIP-OT and ViSigLIP-OT consistently outperform their corresponding baselines across both datasets in zero-shot settings, confirming the generalizability of the SIGROT loss.**\n\n"
md += "| Dataset | Method | Text→Image R@1 | Text→Image R@5 | Text→Image R@10 | Image→Text R@1 | Image→Text R@5 | Image→Text R@10 | Avg. |\n"
md += "|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|\n"
md += "| KTVIC-train | CLIP | 21.12 | 46.99 | 59.22 | 31.65 | 59.46 | 72.49 | 48.49 |\n"
md += "| KTVIC-train | SigLIP | 23.16 | 48.78 | 60.57 | 35.48 | 62.22 | 73.64 | 50.64 |\n"
md += "| KTVIC-train | ViCLIP-OT | 26.24 | 52.46 | 64.14 | 38.47 | 64.37 | 75.48 | 53.52 |\n"
md += "| KTVIC-train | ViSigLIP-OT | 26.28 | 52.58 | 63.49 | 39.62 | 66.44 | 77.78 | 54.37 |\n"
md += "| KTVIC-test | CLIP | 50.32 | 82.80 | 89.94 | 63.06 | 92.36 | 97.45 | 79.32 |\n"
md += "| KTVIC-test | SigLIP | 52.61 | 83.31 | 89.94 | 71.97 | 94.27 | 96.18 | 81.38 |\n"
md += "| KTVIC-test | ViCLIP-OT | 56.69 | 85.61 | 91.97 | 70.06 | 93.63 | 98.09 | 82.68 |\n"
md += "| KTVIC-test | ViSigLIP-OT | 56.56 | 85.99 | 91.72 | 71.34 | 93.63 | 97.45 | 82.78 |\n"
md += "| Crossmodal-3600 | CLIP | 22.52 | 45.55 | 58.01 | 26.22 | 53.42 | 65.06 | 45.13 |\n"
md += "| Crossmodal-3600 | SigLIP | 26.67 | 50.31 | 61.78 | 31.17 | 57.78 | 69.83 | 49.59 |\n"
md += "| Crossmodal-3600 | ViCLIP-OT | 28.90 | 55.29 | 66.37 | 42.56 | 68.81 | 79.17 | 56.85 |\n"
md += "| Crossmodal-3600 | ViSigLIP-OT | 32.04 | 57.90 | 68.95 | 37.97 | 64.64 | 75.53 | 56.17 |\n"
md += "\n"

# Efficient Encoder-Free 3D LMM
md += "#### Efficient Encoder-Free Fourier-based 3D Large Multimodal Model\n\n"
md += "**Table 2: Dense captioning results on ScanRefer and Nr3D. #Param/FLOP: number of activated parameters and Floating Point Operation count required for the encoding/tokenization stage. Best result in bold. Second best result is underlined.**\n\n"
md += "| Method | w/seg | #Params ↓ | FLOP ↓ | ScanRefer C@0.5↑ | ScanRefer B-4@0.5↑ | ScanRefer M@0.5↑ | ScanRefer R@0.5↑ | Nr3D C@0.5↑ | Nr3D B-4@0.5↑ | Nr3D M@0.5↑ | Nr3D R@0.5↑ |\n"
md += "|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|\n"
md += "| **Encoder-based 3D LMMs** | | | | | | | | | | | |\n"
md += "| LL3DA | ✓ | 118.87M | 80.43 | 65.19 | 36.79 | 25.97 | 55.06 | 51.18 | 28.75 | 25.91 | 56.61 |\n"
md += "| PerLA | ✓ | 119.76M | 326.76 | 69.41 | 38.02 | 29.07 | 56.80 | 55.06 | 31.24 | 28.52 | 59.13 |\n"
md += "| 3D-LLaVA | ✓ | 58.26M | 37.75 | 78.80 | 36.90 | 27.10 | 57.70 | - | - | - | - |\n"
md += "| **Encoder-free 3D LMMs** | | | | | | | | | | | |\n"
md += "| Fase3D | ✓ | 10.54M | 2.04 | 78.14 | 41.34 | 27.92 | 57.63 | 54.91 | 30.24 | 26.48 | 57.14 |\n"
md += "| Fase3D | ✗ | 10.54M | 70.72 | 37.83 | 26.81 | 56.37 | 52.89 | 29.31 | 26.14 | 56.41 | - |\n"
md += "\n"

# Write to file
with open('/tmp_workspace/results/paper_digest.md', 'w') as f:
    f.write(md)

print("paper_digest.md written successfully!")
print(f"File size: {len(md)} bytes")
PYEOF

### Caption-Related Papers

#### CapRL: Stimulating Dense Image Caption Capabilities via Reinforcement Learning

**Table 2: Ablation on image sources. We annotate the images in ShareGPT4V-1M and DenseFusion-1M using CapRL-3B, and use them respectively as pretraining datasets for comparison.**

| Pretraining Dataset | Info VQA | Doc VQA | Chart QA | Real WorldQA | Math Vista | SEED2 Plus | MME RW | MMB | MMStar | MMVet | AI2D | GQA | Average |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Vanilla | 43.9 | 81.0 | 72.7 | 55.1 | 41.6 | 56.6 | 30.5 | 68.6 | 44.7 | 41.0 | 68.3 | 61.5 | 55.5 |
| ShareGPT4V-1M | 46.1 | 82.4 | 74.2 | 55.0 | 44.7 | 60.5 | 29.8 | 68.9 | 45.2 | 42.4 | 70.1 | 61.4 | 56.7 |
| CapRL-ShareGPT4V-1M | 52.1 | 85.9 | 75.2 | 56.3 | 45.6 | 60.0 | 30.9 | 70.9 | 46.7 | 47.5 | 71.4 | 61.7 | 58.7 |
| DenseFusion-1M | 49.4 | 84.6 | 74.4 | 54.1 | 44.6 | 59.1 | 30.7 | 69.0 | 45.6 | 40.2 | 70.4 | 62.5 | 57.1 |
| CapRL-DenseFusion-1M | 55.0 | 87.8 | 77.5 | 56.2 | 44.7 | 62.8 | 32.0 | 71.0 | 46.6 | 49.9 | 72.7 | 62.3 | 59.9 |

#### Asymmetric Idiosyncrasies in Multimodal Models

**Table 2: Top distinctive TF-IDF phrases for each captioning model (generic appearance descriptors removed).**

| Rank | Claude-3.5-Sonnet | Gemini-1.5-Pro | GPT-4o | Qwen3-VL |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | lighting suggests | overall impression | image depicts | detailed description |
| 2 | visible background | low resolution | image features | high contrast |
| 3 | light colored | slightly low angle | person wearing | central focus |
| 4 | appears taken | close slightly low | handwritten digit | main subject |
| 5 | photo taken | eye level view | lush green | depth field |
| 6 | lighting creates | high angle view | sunny day | shallow depth field |
| 7 | depth field | light gray | clear blue | black background digit |
| 8 | composition creates | partially visible | handwritten number | white number |
| 9 | overall composition | overall lighting | setting appears | visible elements |
| 10 | scene appears | slightly high angle | partially visible | image captures |

#### Cytoarchitecture in Words: Weakly Supervised Vision-Language Modeling for Human Brain Microscopy

**Note: This paper contains only one table (Table 1). There is no Table 2 in this paper.**

Table 1 shows the performance of LLMs on the created QA dataset for cytoarchitecture, reporting the share of correctly answered multiple choice questions out of 10,955.

| Model | # Parameters | Medical fine-tuning? | Score |
|---|---|---|---|
| First author performance | n.a. | n.a. | 34.0% |
| BioGPT | 0.35b | ✓ | 22.7% |
| BioGPT-Large | 1.5b | ✓ | 24.5% |

| Llama-3-8b | 8b | ✗ | 58.1% |
| Ministral 8b | 8b | ✗ | 50.2% |
| Llama-3-8b Ultramedical | 8b | ✓ | 47.5% |
| Phi-4 | 14b | ✗ | 57.2% |

#### ViCLIP-OT: The First Foundation Vision-Language Model for Vietnamese Image-Text Retrieval with Optimal Transport

**Table 2: Zero-shot image–text retrieval results on KTVIC and Crossmodal-3600. KTVIC images are deduplicated against the UIT-OpenViIC training set. Vietnamese captions are used for Crossmodal-3600. ViCLIP-OT and ViSigLIP-OT consistently outperform their corresponding baselines across both datasets in zero-shot settings, confirming the generalizability of the SIGROT loss.**

| Dataset | Method | Text→Image R@1 | Text→Image R@5 | Text→Image R@10 | Image→Text R@1 | Image→Text R@5 | Image→Text R@10 | Avg. |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| KTVIC-train | CLIP | 21.12 | 46.99 | 59.22 | 31.65 | 59.46 | 72.49 | 48.49 |
| KTVIC-train | SigLIP | 23.16 | 48.78 | 60.57 | 35.48 | 62.22 | 73.64 | 50.64 |
| KTVIC-train | ViCLIP-OT | 26.24 | 52.46 | 64.14 | 38.47 | 64.37 | 75.48 | 53.52 |
| KTVIC-train | ViSigLIP-OT | 26.28 | 52.58 | 63.49 | 39.62 | 66.44 | 77.78 | 54.37 |
| KTVIC-test | CLIP | 50.32 | 82.80 | 89.94 | 63.06 | 92.36 | 97.45 | 79.32 |
| KTVIC-test | SigLIP | 52.61 | 83.31 | 89.94 | 71.97 | 94.27 | 96.18 | 81.38 |
| KTVIC-test | ViCLIP-OT | 56.69 | 85.61 | 91.97 | 70.06 | 93.63 | 98.09 | 82.68 |
| KTVIC-test | ViSigLIP-OT | 56.56 | 85.99 | 91.72 | 71.34 | 93.63 | 97.45 | 82.78 |
| Crossmodal-3600 | CLIP | 22.52 | 45.55 | 58.01 | 26.22 | 53.42 | 65.06 | 45.13 |
| Crossmodal-3600 | SigLIP | 26.67 | 50.31 | 61.78 | 31.17 | 57.78 | 69.83 | 49.59 |
| Crossmodal-3600 | ViCLIP-OT | 28.90 | 55.29 | 66.37 | 42.56 | 68.81 | 79.17 | 56.85 |
| Crossmodal-3600 | ViSigLIP-OT | 32.04 | 57.90 | 68.95 | 37.97 | 64.64 | 75.53 | 56.17 |

|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|

| PerLA | ✓ | 119.76M | 326.76 | 69.41 | 38.02 | 29.07 | 56.80 | 55.06 | 31.24 | 28.52 | 59.13 |
| 3D-LLaVA | ✓ | 58.26M | 37.75 | 78.80 | 36.90 | 27.10 | 57.70 | - | - | - | - |
| **Encoder-free 3D LMMs** | | | | | | | | | | | |
| Fase3D | ✓ | 10.54M | 2.04 | 78.14 | 41.34 | 27.92 | 57.63 | 54.91 | 30.24 | 26.48 | 57.14 |

全部已完成。以下是处理总结：

**所有任务已完成：**

1. **文件重命名**：所有 65 篇 PDF 已根据标题重命名（空格已转换为 `_`，斜杠已转换为 `_`），并放置在 `/tmp_workspace/results/papers/` 目录中，保持扁平结构。

2. **分类**：所有 65 篇论文已分类归入 6 个类别：
   - **多模态 / 视觉语言模型 (Multimodal / Vision-Language Models)**：10 篇论文
   - **医学图像分析 (Medical Image Analysis)**：8 篇论文
   - **图像 / 视频生成与编辑 (Image / Video Generation & Editing)**：19 篇论文
   - **自动驾驶 / 机器人 / 具身智能 (Autonomous Driving / Robotics / Embodied AI)**：3 篇论文
   - **3D 视觉 / 重建 / 高斯泼溅 (3D Vision / Reconstruction / Gaussian Splatting)**：6 篇论文
   - **其他 (Others)**：19 篇论文

3. **与 Caption 相关的论文**：识别出 5 篇与 image caption 相关的论文：
   - **CapRL** — 直接关于通过强化学习进行密集图像 caption
   - **Asymmetric Idiosyncrasies in Multimodal Models** — 研究 caption 模型的特征
   - **Cytoarchitecture in Words** — 生成脑显微图像的 caption（仅有 1 个表格，因此改为提取了 Table 1）
   - **ViCLIP-OT** — 使用图像 caption 数据集进行越南语图像-文本检索
   - **Efficient Encoder-Free 3D LMM** — 在 3D 密集 caption 任务上进行评估

每篇论文的 Table 2（或在论文只有一个表格时使用 Table 1）已提取为 Markdown 表格并包含在 `paper_digest.md` 中。
